chunk.text: BME’s New Quarterly Report on Copper For over ten years now, BME has published a Quarterly Report on Copper. The publication is well respected in the industry as a source of copper data and forecasts. It has provided the only thorough analysis of physical market fundamentals, which shows copper’s transition through all stages of the process chain linked in one integrated database. Over the past year at BME, we have been substantially upgraded our data systems, creating the new CopperChain Database. Summary data and analysis from this revised and expanded data system is already available in our monthly Copper Briefing Service. While retaining the best features of our earlier data systems, our new database allows us to offer a much enhanced service to our clients. In the new Quarterly Report on Copper we give direct access to detailed historical and forecast data on the copper supply chain, both globally and for individual countries and regions. We also provide our view on price, taking into account financial as well as physical market influences.

relations.evidence: Summary data and analysis from this revised and expanded data system is already available in our monthly Copper Briefing Service.

chunk.text: lly and for individual countries and regions. We also provide our view on price, taking into account financial as well as physical market influences. Specifically, we list the features of the Report, indicating what is new or extended in the new edition:  An editorial review and five­year forecast of physical copper market fundamentals and price EXTENDED  A review of copper mining company performance NEW  A news round up for the previous three months NEW  Global and summary tables showing capacity, output, trade and stocks of refined copper products and all other products along the copper process chain EXTENDED  Detailed country and region tables covering over 60 countries, showing production capacities and all process chain items as described above EXTENDED  An Excel Client File containing the full CopperChain data set, currently covering the years 2000 to 2015 NEW  Telephone or email access to the Editor of the Quarterly, who will be available to answer your queries Simultaneously with the release of the new Quarterly, BME is also launching its updated Interactive Copper Price Model. The new product will integrate closely with the physical

relations.evidence: A review of copper mining company performance NEW

chunk.text: market fundamentals analysis in our CopperChain database. The Model looks not only at the physical market but also the investor­driven futures market, and the interplay between the two in determining price. The Editorial Team of the Quarterly will utilise the Model to explore alternative price scenarios, but its primary purpose is to allow subscribers to make their own scenarios. What, for example, would be the impact on price of a certain volume of ETFs, given alternative physical market conditions? As such, we see a great advantage in owning and using the Quarterly Report and the Model side­by side. We offer subscribers that wish to do this a package discount. To give you a flavour of the new Quarterly Report on Copper, we enclose a few tables taken from the issue of the Report published in October 2010. Prices for the Quarterly Report on Copper and the Interactive Copper Price Model Quarterly Report on Copper £5,000 Interactive Copper Price Model £10,000 Combined Price, Quarterly & Price Model £13,500 For more details on either of these services please contact initially: Robert Goldstein: rbg@bloomsburyminerals.com
